graph.topic: Exploring workflows, cognitive decision-making, and tooling challenges in computational cell state discovery
graph.researcher_bg: An HCI and Visualization (VIS) researcher specializing in human-in-the-loop bioinformatics. Focused on understanding the cognitive load, analytical bottlenecks, and the reliance on intuition when computational biologists analyze high-dimensional single-cell data. The goal is to design better visual analytics systems that bridge the gap between algorithmic clustering and biological interpretation
graph.expert_bg: An experienced computational biologist or bioinformatician heavily involved in single-cell RNA-seq or spatial transcriptomics data analysis. Proficient in tools like Seurat or Scanpy, they frequently navigate the ambiguity of defining, interpreting, and validating novel cell states from complex datasets, balancing statistical signals with biological intuition and wet-lab validation requirements

q_01__shared_root.label: Exploring workflows, cognitive decision-making, and tooling challenges in computational cell state discovery
q_01__shared_root.description: Interview topic: Exploring workflows, cognitive decision-making, and tooling challenges in computational cell state discovery

q_01__exp_L1_00.label: Navigating Computational Ambiguity
q_01__exp_L1_00.description: Dealing with the mathematical artifacts and parameter sensitivities that mimic biological signals during initial processing

q_01__exp_L2_00_00.label: Clustering Dynamics & Stability
q_01__exp_L2_00_00.description: Managing how algorithms partition cells into groups and ensuring those groups aren't just math artifacts

q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_00.label: Resolution Parameter Tuning
q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_00.description: You run the clustering, but the default setting never works. You're constantly nudging that resolution number up or down until the splits feel biologically real rather than just mathematical noise.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_01.label: Rare Population vs. Doublet Filtering
q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_01.description: When you see a tiny cluster of 20 cells, you have to decide if it's a rare new cell type or just two cells stuck together. The software gives a score, but you often override it based on gene expression patterns.

q_01__exp_L2_00_01.label: Visualization Trust
q_01__exp_L2_00_01.description: Interpreting 2D projections without being misled by dimensionality reduction distortions

q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_00.label: UMAP Proximity Misleading
q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_00.description: It's tempting to think two dots close together on the UMAP are related, but sometimes the algorithm squishes distant populations together. You have to constantly check back against the high-dimensional distance matrix.

q_01__exp_L1_01.label: Biological Meaning-Making
q_01__exp_L1_01.description: The cognitive process of translating gene lists into functional cell identities and recognizing novelty

q_01__exp_L2_01_00.label: Marker Gene Interpretation
q_01__exp_L2_01_00.description: Evaluating whether differential expression results actually represent a coherent biological state

q_01__exp_leaf_01_00_00.label: Recognizing Program Coherence
q_01__exp_leaf_01_00_00.description: When you look at the top marker genes, you need to sense if they tell a story together. If it's a random mix of housekeeping genes, it's junk. If they pathway-align, you're onto something. This relies heavily on experience and pattern recognition.

q_01__exp_leaf_01_00_01.label: Distinguishing Gradients from States
q_01__exp_leaf_01_00_01.description: Sometimes cells aren't in a new box, they're just moving along a trajectory. You have to decide if you're looking at a discrete new state or just cells halfway through activation, often using trajectory analysis to back it up.

q_01__exp_L2_01_01.label: Taxonomy Alignment
q_01__exp_L2_01_01.description: Reconciling new findings with existing cell type nomenclature and literature

q_01__exp_leaf_01_01_00.label: Annotation & Identity Mismatch
q_01__exp_leaf_01_01_00.description: You find a cluster that doesn't fit existing labels or literature. You spend time figuring out if you're actually new or just using different words, often cross-referencing other datasets to confirm the mismatch.

q_01__exp_L1_02.label: Validation & Confidence
q_01__exp_L1_02.description: Closing the loop between computational prediction and experimental proof while managing risk

q_01__exp_L2_02_00.label: Experimental Translation
q_01__exp_L2_02_00.description: Selecting the right computational findings to test in the wet lab given limited resources

q_01__exp_leaf_02_00_00.label: Marker Panel Selection
q_01__exp_leaf_02_00_00.description: You can't validate 50 genes. You pick the top 3 that are clean and distinct, knowing that if those fail, the whole hypothesis might collapse, even if the RNA-seq data looked great.

q_01__exp_L2_02_01.label: Claiming Novelty
q_01__exp_L2_02_01.description: Determining the threshold of evidence required to publicly assert a new cell state discovery

q_01__exp_leaf_02_01_00.label: The 'Bet Your Reputation' Check
q_01__exp_leaf_02_01_00.description: There's no p-value for 'novelty.' You just have to feel sure enough that when you present this at a conference, people won't tear it apart as an artifact. It's a gut check on risk before going to the lab.

q_01__res_leaf_00_00_01.description: Quantify how often analysts cycle between preprocessing and clustering before feeling ready to interpret.

q_01__res_L2_00_01.label: Effort-Insight Ratio
q_01__res_L2_00_01.description: Understanding the relationship between cognitive exertion and analytical value across different pipeline stages.

q_01__res_leaf_00_01_00.label: Pinpoint high-load low-value steps
q_01__res_leaf_00_01_00.description: Locate workflow segments that demand high cognitive load but yield low perceived discovery value.

q_01__res_L1_01.label: Tacit Knowledge & Heuristic Application
q_01__res_L1_01.description: Investigating where and how human intuition overrides or supplements algorithmic outputs during analysis.

q_01__res_L2_01_00.label: Intuition Triggers
q_01__res_L2_01_00.description: Identifying the specific conditions that prompt an analyst to switch from automated to manual reasoning.

q_01__res_leaf_01_00_00.label: Detect algo-to-manual switch points
q_01__res_leaf_01_00_00.description: Identify workflow stages where analysts switch from algorithmic reliance to manual curation based on suspicion.

q_01__res_leaf_01_00_01.label: Articulate gut feelings
q_01__res_leaf_01_00_01.description: Explore how analysts describe unquantifiable instincts when formal metrics are ambiguous.

q_01__res_L2_01_01.label: Rule Flexibility
q_01__res_L2_01_01.description: Understanding how standard operating procedures are adapted based on expert experience.

q_01__res_leaf_01_01_00.label: Map protocol deviations
q_01__res_leaf_01_01_00.description: Document where expert-specific shortcuts diverge from published pipeline protocols.

q_01__res_L1_02.label: Phenomenology of Discovery
q_01__res_L1_02.description: Characterizing the subjective experience and cognitive state associated with the moment of 'actual discovery'.

q_01__res_L2_02_00.label: The Discovery Event
q_01__res_L2_02_00.description: Defining the sensory and cognitive modality where the 'Aha!' moment typically occurs.

q_01__res_leaf_02_00_00.label: Characterize discovery modality
q_01__res_leaf_02_00_00.description: Determine if the moment of insight is primarily visual, numerical, or collaborative in nature.

q_01__res_leaf_02_00_01.label: Distinguish cluster vs. state
q_01__res_leaf_02_00_01.description: Clarify the conceptual boundary between finding a computational cluster and biologically interpreting it as a state.

q_01__res_L2_02_01.label: Internal Validation
q_01__res_L2_02_01.description: Understanding the subjective confidence required before seeking external proof.

q_01__res_leaf_02_01_00.label: Define confidence thresholds
q_01__res_leaf_02_01_00.description: Identify the subjective confidence level required before initiating formal validation protocols.

pivot.limitation: Focusing solely on parameter adjustment overlooks whether analysts ever revert to raw data during these iterations. The researcher risks missing critical workflow breakpoints where processed objects are abandoned.
pivot.research_goal: The researcher aims to identify specific stages where analysts backtrack to raw data versus moving forward with processed objects. This determines the process granularity and transition points.
pivot.compelling_reason: While the expert prioritizes parameter tuning for biological validity, the researcher requires insight into raw data reversion to map workflow resilience. Both the expert's stability concerns and the researcher's transition goals are vital for capturing the full scope of computational ambiguity.
pivot.coarse_scenario: I see how much effort you put into nudging these resolution numbers, but I also need to understand if there are moments where you decide to throw that work out and return to the raw data.

q_01__shared_root.label: Exploring workflows, cognitive decision-making, and tooling challenges in computational cell state discovery
q_01__shared_root.description: Interview topic: Exploring workflows, cognitive decision-making, and tooling challenges in computational cell state discovery

q_01__exp_L1_00.label: Navigating Computational Ambiguity
q_01__exp_L1_00.description: Dealing with the mathematical artifacts and parameter sensitivities that mimic biological signals during initial processing

q_01__exp_L2_00_00.label: Clustering Dynamics & Stability
q_01__exp_L2_00_00.description: Managing how algorithms partition cells into groups and ensuring those groups aren't just math artifacts

q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_00.label: Resolution Parameter Tuning
q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_00.description: You run the clustering, but the default setting never works. You're constantly nudging that resolution number up or down until the splits feel biologically real rather than just mathematical noise.

q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_01.label: Rare Population vs. Doublet Filtering
q_01__exp_leaf_00_00_01.description: When you see a tiny cluster of 20 cells, you have to decide if it's a rare new cell type or just two cells stuck together. The software gives a score, but you often override it based on gene expression patterns.

q_01__exp_L2_00_01.label: Visualization Trust
q_01__exp_L2_00_01.description: Interpreting 2D projections without being misled by dimensionality reduction distortions

q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_00.label: UMAP Proximity Misleading
q_01__exp_leaf_00_01_00.description: It's tempting to think two dots close together on the UMAP are related, but sometimes the algorithm squishes distant populations together. You have to constantly check back against the high-dimensional distance matrix.

q_01__exp_L1_01.label: Biological Meaning-Making
q_01__exp_L1_01.description: The cognitive process of translating gene lists into functional cell identities and recognizing novelty

q_01__exp_L2_01_00.label: Marker Gene Interpretation
q_01__exp_L2_01_00.description: Evaluating whether differential expression results actually represent a coherent biological state

q_01__exp_leaf_01_00_00.label: Recognizing Program Coherence
q_01__exp_leaf_01_00_00.description: When you look at the top marker genes, you need to sense if they tell a story together. If it's a random mix of housekeeping genes, it's junk. If they pathway-align, you're onto something. This relies heavily on experience and pattern recognition.

q_01__exp_leaf_01_00_01.label: Distinguishing Gradients from States
q_01__exp_leaf_01_00_01.description: Sometimes cells aren't in a new box, they're just moving along a trajectory. You have to decide if you're looking at a discrete new state or just cells halfway through activation, often using trajectory analysis to back it up.

q_01__exp_L2_01_01.label: Taxonomy Alignment
q_01__exp_L2_01_01.description: Reconciling new findings with existing cell type nomenclature and literature

q_01__exp_leaf_01_01_00.label: Annotation & Identity Mismatch
q_01__exp_leaf_01_01_00.description: You find a cluster that doesn't fit existing labels or literature. You spend time figuring out if you're actually new or just using different words, often cross-referencing other datasets to confirm the mismatch.

q_01__exp_L1_02.label: Validation & Confidence
q_01__exp_L1_02.description: Closing the loop between computational prediction and experimental proof while managing risk

q_01__exp_L2_02_00.label: Experimental Translation
q_01__exp_L2_02_00.description: Selecting the right computational findings to test in the wet lab given limited resources

q_01__exp_leaf_02_00_00.label: Marker Panel Selection
q_01__exp_leaf_02_00_00.description: You can't validate 50 genes. You pick the top 3 that are clean and distinct, knowing that if those fail, the whole hypothesis might collapse, even if the RNA-seq data looked great.

q_01__exp_L2_02_01.label: Claiming Novelty
q_01__exp_L2_02_01.description: Determining the threshold of evidence required to publicly assert a new cell state discovery

q_01__exp_leaf_02_01_00.label: The 'Bet Your Reputation' Check
q_01__exp_leaf_02_01_00.description: There's no p-value for 'novelty.' You just have to feel sure enough that when you present this at a conference, people won't tear it apart as an artifact. It's a gut check on risk before going to the lab.

q_02__exp_L2_00_00.label: Resolution & Clustering Robustness
q_02__exp_L2_00_00.description: Strategies to verify that cell groups persist across different parameter settings

q_02__exp_leaf_00_00_00.label: Consensus Clustering Stability
q_02__exp_leaf_00_00_00.description: You run the clustering 50 times with subsampling to see if the same cells stick together every time, because if they shuffle around, it's probably noise.

q_02__exp_leaf_00_00_01.label: Resolution Sensitivity Profiling
q_02__exp_leaf_00_00_01.description: You don't just pick 0.8; you sweep from 0.4 to 1.2 and watch where the branch points stabilize before committing to a split.

q_02__exp_L2_00_01.label: Artifact & Noise Filtering
q_02__exp_L2_00_01.description: Identifying when clusters are driven by artifacts rather than biology

q_02__exp_leaf_00_01_00.label: Batch-Driven Split Detection
q_02__exp_leaf_00_01_00.description: You check if the cluster is just all from Patient A or one specific sequencing lane, because that's usually a batch effect pretending to be a cell type.

q_02__exp_leaf_00_01_01.label: Small Cluster Doublet Skepticism
q_02__exp_leaf_00_01_01.description: Wary of small clusters unless they have strong markers; usually assume a tiny group of 20 cells is a doublet or noise until proven otherwise.

q_02__exp_leaf_00_01_02.label: Visualization Shape Heuristic
q_02__exp_leaf_00_01_02.description: If it's just a weird blob in UMAP with no clear boundary or it looks like a noisy smear, I usually don't trust it as a distinct state.

q_02__exp_L1_01.label: Biological & Contextual Validation
q_02__exp_L1_01.description: Integrating spatial, morphological, and regulatory data to validate transcriptomic states

q_02__exp_L2_01_00.label: Spatial & Morphological Concordance
q_02__exp_L2_01_00.description: Using physical location and cell shape to confirm gene expression profiles

q_02__exp_leaf_01_00_00.label: Spatial Neighborhood Validation
q_02__exp_leaf_01_00_00.description: You look at the tissue map to see if they're clumped in a specific region or spread out. If they're in a distinct location, that adds weight.

q_02__exp_leaf_01_00_01.label: Morphology-Transcriptome Alignment
q_02__exp_leaf_01_00_01.description: If you have imaging, you check if the cell shape actually looks like the gene profile says it should, like a neuron looking neuronal.

q_02__exp_L2_01_01.label: Marker & Regulatory Coherence
q_02__exp_L2_01_01.description: Evaluating whether gene lists and regulatory logic represent a coherent biological state

q_02__exp_leaf_01_01_00.label: Pathway & Marker Story Coherence
q_02__exp_leaf_01_01_00.description: You check if the top genes make a biological story together. If it's a random mix of housekeeping genes, it's junk. If they pathway-align, you're onto something.

q_02__exp_leaf_01_01_01.label: Transcription Factor Logic Hierarchy
q_02__exp_leaf_01_01_01.description: When surface markers say 'T-cell' but the transcription factors scream 'Macrophage,' you trust the TFs because they drive the state.

q_02__exp_L1_02.label: Cognitive Heuristics & Novelty
q_02__exp_L1_02.description: Tacit rules of thumb used to flag potential discoveries versus artifacts

q_02__exp_L2_02_00.label: Unexpected Biological Signals
q_02__exp_L2_02_00.description: Recognizing gene co-expression patterns that violate established lineage dogmas

q_02__exp_leaf_02_00_00.label: Mutually Exclusive Marker Co-expression
q_02__exp_leaf_02_00_00.description: Seeing CD4 and CD8 on the same cell usually means doublet, but sometimes your gut says it's a real transition state worth chasing.

q_02__exp_L2_02_01.label: Reproducibility & Intuition
q_02__exp_L2_02_01.description: Verifying findings in independent datasets and relying on experience-based pattern recognition

q_02__exp_leaf_02_01_00.label: Cross-Dataset Recurrence
q_02__exp_leaf_02_01_00.description: You can't claim novelty until you find the same weird cluster in a public dataset, otherwise it's just your samples acting up.

q_02__exp_leaf_02_01_01.label: Experience-Based Pattern Recognition
q_02__exp_leaf_02_01_01.description: After seeing many datasets, you recognize what real biology looks like versus noise. It's what feels right when the data doesn't clearly tell you.

q_02__shared_root.label: Exploring workflows, cognitive decision-making, and tooling challenges in computational cell state discovery
q_02__shared_root.description: Interview topic: Exploring workflows, cognitive decision-making, and tooling challenges in computational cell state discovery

q_02__res_L1_00.label: Cognitive Strategies for Parameter Tuning
q_02__res_L1_00.description: Investigating the mental models and cognitive load associated with adjusting algorithmic clustering parameters.

q_02__res_L2_00_00.label: Mental Models of Resolution
q_02__res_L2_00_00.description: Understanding how experts conceptually map abstract algorithmic parameters to biological hierarchies.

q_02__res_leaf_00_00_00.label: Biological Interpretation of Parameters
q_02__res_leaf_00_00_00.description: Investigate how experts map the abstract 'resolution' parameter to concrete biological hierarchies (e.g., subtype vs. state) to form a mental model for tuning.

q_02__res_leaf_00_00_01.label: Visual Stopping Criteria
q_02__res_leaf_00_00_01.description: Identify the specific visual or statistical triggers that serve as 'stop signals' during iterative parameter adjustment to prevent over-splitting.

q_02__res_L2_00_01.label: Working Memory in Comparative Analysis
q_02__res_L2_00_01.description: Examining the cognitive strain of tracking cluster identity stability across multiple parameter runs.

q_02__res_leaf_00_01_00.label: Tracking Cluster Stability
q_02__res_leaf_00_01_00.description: Assess the cognitive load required to mentally track cluster identity stability when resolution parameters shift between runs.

q_02__res_leaf_00_01_01.label: Context Anchoring Techniques
q_02__res_leaf_00_01_01.description: Explore techniques experts use to anchor their decision-making context when comparing multiple clustering iterations to maintain orientation.

q_02__res_L1_01.label: Heuristics for Novelty and State Boundaries
q_02__res_L1_01.description: Exploring the rules of thumb and perceptual judgments used to distinguish new states from artifacts or continua.

q_02__res_L2_01_00.label: Multimodal Signal Conflict Resolution
q_02__res_L2_01_00.description: Analyzing how experts weigh conflicting evidence from different data modalities during hypothesis formation.

q_02__res_leaf_01_00_00.label: Hierarchy of Signal Trust
q_02__res_leaf_01_00_00.description: Understand the hierarchy of trust when transcriptomic clusters conflict with spatial or morphological signals during state identification.

q_02__res_leaf_01_00_01.label: Thresholds for Weak Signals
q_02__res_leaf_01_00_01.description: Determine the minimum signal strength required for a marker gene to influence a novelty hypothesis when expression is ambiguous.

q_02__res_L2_01_01.label: Perceptual Judgment of Continuity
q_02__res_L2_01_01.description: Investigating the visual cues that lead experts to discretize continuous data into distinct cell states.

q_02__res_leaf_01_01_00.label: Visual Segmentation Triggers
q_02__res_leaf_01_01_00.description: Identify visual patterns in dimensionality reduction that prompt an expert to 'cut' a continuous trajectory into discrete states.

q_02__res_leaf_01_01_01.label: Boundary Cell Classification
q_02__res_leaf_01_01_01.description: Examine how experts classify cells that exhibit mixed markers lying between defined clusters to manage definition ambiguity.

q_03__exp_L1_00.label: Evidence Thresholds for Novelty
q_03__exp_L1_00.description: Determining the weight of different data types before committing to a discovery claim or wet-lab investment

q_03__exp_L2_00_00.label: Computational Conviction
q_03__exp_L2_00_00.description: Internal statistical and biological signals that build confidence in a new state

q_03__exp_leaf_00_00_00.label: Marker & Program Coherence
q_03__exp_leaf_00_00_00.description: It's not just GO terms; the markers need to tell a biological story together. If it's a random mix of housekeeping genes, I don't trust it as a new state.

q_03__exp_leaf_00_00_01.label: External Replication Necessity
q_03__exp_leaf_00_00_01.description: If I can't find this cluster in a public atlas or another dataset, I'm skeptical. It needs to show up twice before I call it real.

q_03__exp_leaf_00_00_02.label: Resolution Stability Check
q_03__exp_leaf_00_00_02.description: I sweep the resolution parameters. If the cluster disappears at 0.6 or 1.0, it's probably noise. It needs to persist across settings.

q_03__exp_L2_00_01.label: Experimental Translation Risk
q_03__exp_L2_00_01.description: Assessing the feasibility and likelihood of wet-lab confirmation before committing resources

q_03__exp_leaf_00_01_00.label: Wet-Lab Risk Assessment
q_03__exp_leaf_00_01_00.description: Before committing resources, I ask if I'd be embarrassed if it failed. If I'm not sure, I drop it. I only push forward when I'd be shocked if it failed.

q_03__exp_L1_01.label: Iterative Cluster Remediation
q_03__exp_L1_01.description: Manual interventions and decision points when initial clustering fails to align with biological reality

q_03__exp_L2_01_00.label: Topology Adjustment
q_03__exp_L2_01_00.description: Merging or splitting clusters based on marker inspection rather than algorithmic output

q_03__exp_leaf_01_00_00.label: Biologically-Guided Merging
q_03__exp_leaf_01_00_00.description: Sometimes math splits what's biologically one. I override and merge them manually if markers are identical, regardless of the algorithm.

q_03__exp_leaf_01_00_01.label: Trajectory vs State Discrimination
q_03__exp_leaf_01_00_01.description: Is it a new state or just cells moving along a gradient? I run trajectory analysis to check before calling it novel.

q_03__exp_L2_01_01.label: Artifact & Context Filtering
q_03__exp_L2_01_01.description: Identifying and removing clusters that represent noise, stress, or impossible biology

q_03__exp_leaf_01_01_00.label: Batch & Technical Artifact Filtering
q_03__exp_leaf_01_01_00.description: I check if the cluster is just one patient or one lane. High mitochondrial genes also flag dying cells, not new types. I filter these early.

q_03__exp_leaf_01_01_01.label: Tissue Context Plausibility
q_03__exp_leaf_01_01_01.description: If liver data shows neuronal markers, I assume contamination. The tissue origin has to make sense before I believe the markers.

q_03__exp_L2_01_02.label: Cognitive Heuristics
q_03__exp_L2_01_02.description: Tacit rules of thumb used to manage ambiguity and decision fatigue during analysis

q_03__exp_leaf_01_02_00.label: Cognitive Pause & Fresh Eyes
q_03__exp_leaf_01_02_00.description: If it doesn't feel right, I let it sit. Coming back with fresh eyes often reveals if I'm forcing a pattern or missing something obvious.

q_03__res_L1_00.label: Epistemology of Computational Evidence
q_03__res_L1_00.description: Understanding how experts weigh different types of data to establish belief in a new cell state before experimental validation

q_03__res_L2_00_00.label: Evidence Weighting & Conflict Resolution
q_03__res_L2_00_00.description: How experts resolve contradictions between mathematical clustering and biological intuition

q_03__res_leaf_00_00_00.label: Trust Calibration between Topology and Expression
q_03__res_leaf_00_00_00.description: Investigate how experts decide when to trust gene expression markers over visualization topology (e.g., UMAP shape) when the two signals contradict each other.

q_03__res_leaf_00_00_01.label: Weight of External Replication vs. Internal Consistency
q_03__res_leaf_00_00_01.description: Determine how much evidence weight is assigned to finding a cluster in public datasets versus having a coherent story within the current experiment alone.

q_03__res_L2_00_01.label: Risk Thresholds for Validation
q_03__res_L2_00_01.description: The criteria used to justify the cost and risk of pursuing a computational finding in the wet lab

q_03__res_leaf_00_01_00.label: Cost-Benefit Threshold for Wet-Lab Pursuit
q_03__res_leaf_00_01_00.description: Identify the minimum combination of computational signals required to justify the expensive and time-consuming process of experimental validation.

q_03__res_leaf_00_01_01.label: Community Standards for Believable Novelty
q_03__res_leaf_00_01_01.description: Explore the implicit social or publication standards that define 'enough' evidence to publicly claim a new cell state without being dismissed as an artifact.

q_03__res_L1_01.label: Human-in-the-Loop Cluster Refinement
q_03__res_L1_01.description: Examining the cognitive strategies and tooling needs when experts manually adjust clusters due to biological mismatch

q_03__res_L2_01_00.label: Manual Intervention Logic
q_03__res_L2_01_00.description: The heuristics and triggers that lead experts to manually override algorithmic outputs

q_03__res_leaf_01_00_00.description: Understand when an expert chooses to manually move specific cells versus re-running the entire clustering pipeline with new parameters.

q_03__res_leaf_01_00_01.label: Provenance of Manual Cluster Modifications
q_03__res_leaf_01_00_01.description: Investigate how experts track the rationale behind manual merges or splits over multiple iterations to avoid losing the reasoning trail.

q_03__res_leaf_01_00_02.label: Cognitive Load of Tracking Cluster Versions
q_03__res_leaf_01_00_02.description: Assess the mental burden of maintaining context about why a cluster looks different from the previous iteration during iterative refinement.

q_03__res_L2_01_01.description: Desired tool features that support rapid testing of biological coherence during cluster adjustment

q_03__res_leaf_01_01_00.description: Explore the need for immediate updates on pathway enrichment or marker scores when a user manually merges or splits clusters.

q_04__shared_root.label: Exploring workflows, cognitive decision-making, and tooling challenges in computational cell state discovery
q_04__shared_root.description: Interview topic: Exploring workflows, cognitive decision-making, and tooling challenges in computational cell state discovery

q_04__exp_L1_00.label: Tooling Friction & Workflow Fluidity
q_04__exp_L1_00.description: The day-to-day experience of using standard packages like Seurat or Scanpy, distinguishing between automated ease and manual drag.

q_04__exp_L2_00_00.label: Routine vs. Exception Handling
q_04__exp_L2_00_00.description: Differentiating between the standardized pipelines that work out-of-the-box and the manual interventions that break flow.

q_04__exp_leaf_00_00_00.label: The 'Happy Path' Comfort
q_04__exp_leaf_00_00_00.description: Running the standard QC and plotting pipeline is buttery smooth; you know exactly which functions to call, and the plots render instantly without thinking.

q_04__exp_leaf_00_00_01.label: Parameter Tuning Grind
q_04__exp_leaf_00_00_01.description: The tedious back-and-forth of tweaking resolution and clustering parameters; you're constantly fighting the software's defaults to make the splits feel biologically real.

q_04__exp_L2_00_01.label: Pipeline Sensitivity
q_04__exp_L2_00_01.description: How small changes in early processing steps cascade into major differences in final cell state definitions.

q_04__exp_leaf_00_01_00.label: The Butterfly Effect of Preprocessing
q_04__exp_leaf_00_01_00.description: Changing a single filtering threshold at the start can completely reshape your UMAP at the end, making you question if any of your findings are robust.

q_04__exp_leaf_00_01_01.label: Algorithmic Ambiguity
q_04__exp_leaf_00_01_01.description: Trying to hold a conversation with a system that speaks in partial sentences; ensuring the biology isn't just a mirage from the math.

q_04__exp_L1_01.label: The Cognitive Burden of Discovery
q_04__exp_L1_01.description: The mental load associated with interpreting computational outputs and making high-stakes decisions about cell identity.

q_04__exp_L2_01_00.label: Interpretation Ambiguity
q_04__exp_L2_01_00.description: Navigating the gap between statistical outputs and coherent biological narratives.

q_04__exp_leaf_01_00_00.label: Statistical Significance vs. Biological Relevance
q_04__exp_leaf_01_00_00.description: You get a low p-value for a cluster marker, but your gut says it's just stress response genes; deciding when to trust the stats over the story is exhausting.

q_04__exp_leaf_01_00_01.label: Discovery vs. Delusion
q_04__exp_leaf_01_00_01.description: Balancing on a knife's edge between finding something new and realizing it's just noise; that uncertainty is what keeps you up at night.

q_04__exp_L2_01_01.label: Decision Fatigue
q_04__exp_L2_01_01.description: The cumulative toll of making hundreds of micro-decisions during the analysis process.

q_04__exp_leaf_01_01_00.label: The Iteration Loop
q_04__exp_leaf_01_01_00.description: The hardest part is the constant backtracking; every decision feels like a tightrope walk between what the data says and what you know it should say.

q_04__exp_leaf_01_01_01.label: The 'Stop Rule' Dilemma
q_04__exp_leaf_01_01_01.description: There's no algorithm to tell you when you've dug deep enough; you have to feel when further sub-clustering is just chasing noise rather than finding truth.

q_04__shared_root.label: Exploring workflows, cognitive decision-making, and tooling challenges in computational cell state discovery
q_04__shared_root.description: Interview topic: Exploring workflows, cognitive decision-making, and tooling challenges in computational cell state discovery

q_04__res_L1_00.label: Tooling Friction & Interaction Efficiency
q_04__res_L1_00.description: Investigating the usability gaps and interaction costs within current bioinformatics platforms that contribute to the 'tedious' aspects of analysis.

q_04__res_L2_00_00.label: Interaction Modalities & Flow
q_04__res_L2_00_00.description: Examining how the interface design supports or disrupts the analyst's concentration and workflow continuity.

q_04__res_leaf_00_00_00.label: Quantifying Flow State Disruptions
q_04__res_leaf_00_00_00.description: Identify specific moments where switching between code editors, console outputs, and visualization panes breaks the analyst's concentration or causes context switching errors.

q_04__res_leaf_00_00_01.label: Feedback Loop Latency
q_04__res_leaf_00_00_01.description: Determine if the delay between parameter adjustment and visual feedback contributes to perceived tediousness or encourages risky 'batch' changes.

q_04__res_L2_00_01.label: Data State & Provenance Management
q_04__res_L2_00_01.description: Exploring the difficulties in tracking, comparing, and managing multiple versions of clustering results within the tool environment.

q_04__res_leaf_00_01_00.label: Visual Provenance Gaps
q_04__res_leaf_00_01_00.description: Understand the friction involved in retracing steps to compare previous clustering iterations side-by-side without manual file management.

q_04__res_leaf_00_01_01.label: Intermediate State Preservation
q_04__res_leaf_00_01_01.description: Assess the tediousness of manually annotating or saving intermediate analysis states to prevent loss of promising but unverified leads.

q_04__res_L1_01.label: Cognitive Load & Decision Sustainability
q_04__res_L1_01.description: Analyzing the mental burden associated with the 'hardest parts' of discovery, specifically focusing on uncertainty, iteration fatigue, and tool trust.

q_04__res_L2_01_00.label: Iterative Fatigue & Stopping Rules
q_04__res_L2_01_00.description: Investigating the cognitive limits that dictate when an analyst stops iterating, distinguishing between biological confidence and mental exhaustion.

q_04__res_leaf_01_00_00.label: Satisficing vs. Optimizing Thresholds
q_04__res_leaf_01_00_00.description: Identify the point where analysis stops due to mental exhaustion rather than biological confidence, and what tool cues might support better stopping decisions.

q_04__res_leaf_01_00_01.label: Working Memory Overload
q_04__res_leaf_01_00_01.description: Explore the burden of maintaining multiple competing hypotheses in working memory during long iterative sessions without external scaffolding.

q_04__res_L2_01_01.label: Algorithmic Trust & Error Attribution
q_04__res_L2_01_01.description: Understanding how analysts diagnose failures during analysis and whether they attribute errors to biological noise or tool limitations.

q_04__res_leaf_01_01_00.label: Source Attribution Difficulty
q_04__res_leaf_01_01_00.description: Determine the cognitive effort required to diagnose whether a poor result stems from biological noise or tool parameter sensitivity during exploration.

q_04__res_leaf_01_01_01.label: Manual Override Frustration
q_04__res_leaf_01_01_01.description: Assess frustration levels when algorithmic defaults consistently require manual intervention to be useful, and how this impacts long-term tool reliance.

_meta.topic: Exploring workflows, cognitive decision-making, and tooling challenges in computational cell state discovery
_meta.researcher_bg: An HCI and Visualization (VIS) researcher specializing in human-in-the-loop bioinformatics. Focused on understanding the cognitive load, analytical bottlenecks, and the reliance on intuition when computational biologists analyze high-dimensional single-cell data. The goal is to design better visual analytics systems that bridge the gap between algorithmic clustering and biological interpretation
_meta.expert_bg: An experienced computational biologist or bioinformatician heavily involved in single-cell RNA-seq or spatial transcriptomics data analysis. Proficient in tools like Seurat or Scanpy, they frequently navigate the ambiguity of defining, interpreting, and validating novel cell states from complex datasets, balancing statistical signals with biological intuition and wet-lab validation requirements
